target.name: Lyten
target.founder: Dan Cook, Bharat Sastri, Mike Stuart

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.3B total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Lyten is a Series C materials-science company founded by Dan Cook, Bharat Sastri, and Mike Stuart. The company has raised $1.33B to date. The comparable universe for advanced materials is limited; only one relevant private peer (Noveon Magnetics) could be identified, with no directly comparable public companies available for benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the primary applicable approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($1.33B) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-later-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $2.3B. This reflects the expected value creation between Series C and subsequent financing rounds in the materials sector, absent broader comparable multiples.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B (low) to $2.6B (high), with a median of $2.3B. Key uncertainties include the limited comparables universe—comprising only one private peer and zero public benchmarks—which constrains validation of stage-progression assumptions. Sector-specific dynamics around commercialization timelines, IP defensibility, and path to revenue remain material variables not captured in this quantitative framework. Additional data on burn rate, runway, and revenue trajectory (if applicable) would strengthen conviction in both the range and specific placement within it.
